definition: Top-level ODPC portfolio container for organizing product references, use cases, business objectives, signals, and catalog metadata such as ownership, scope, lifecycle status, tags, and graph implementation reference.
doUseFor: Domain, audience, ownership, and lifecycle scope
Pointing to an ODPG or other graph implementation through metadata.graph

definition: Lightweight catalog reference to a data product that identifies the product and points to the authoritative product definition through productModel.
doUseFor: Listing data products in a catalog
Referencing ODPS or another authoritative product model
doNotUseFor: Defining access, SLA, pricing, support, or technical interfaces
Connecting products to use cases directly

definition: Demand-side ODPC object describing why data is needed, who needs it, what decision or process it supports, and what outcome is expected.
doUseFor: Business, operational, analytical, policy, or user needs
Explaining expected outcomes and data needs
doNotUseFor: Strategic objectives that own KPI targets
Explicit product relationships or graph edges

definition: Outcome-oriented ODPC object describing a higher-level business, operational, policy, or strategic objective that use cases and data products can contribute to.
doUseFor: Strategic, policy, business, or operational outcomes
doNotUseFor: Graph relationships between objectives and products

definition: Key performance indicator nested inside BusinessObjective.kpis to measure progress toward a business objective.
Observed market, operational, user, technology, policy, competitive, quality, usage, risk, or gap indicator that may create demand for use cases, data products, or product improvements.
doUseFor: Capturing evidence of demand, risk, opportunity, quality, usage, or gaps
doNotUseFor: Direct relationships to products or use cases
